title: RNA-Seq Differential Gene Expression Analysis Workflow

biological_source_material.description: Biological source, experimental conditions, and replicate structure.

tissue_or_cell_type.description: Tissue or cell type from which RNA was extracted.

treatment_condition.description: Experimental condition applied to the biological sample.

biological_replicates.description: Number of independent biological replicates per condition.

sample_collection_protocol.description: Method used to collect and handle samples prior to RNA extraction.

rna_extraction_and_quality_metrics.description: RNA isolation protocol and quality control measurements.

rin.description: RNA Integrity Number score on a 0-10 scale.

quantification_method.description: Instrument or method used to measure RNA concentration.

method.description: RNA enrichment or depletion strategy used for library preparation.

strandedness.description: Whether strand-of-origin information is preserved during library synthesis.

fragmentation_strategy.description: Method used to fragment RNA prior to cDNA synthesis.

amplification_cycles.description: Number of PCR amplification cycles applied during library preparation.

umi_details.description: Unique Molecular Identifier configuration. Only applicable when UMIs are incorporated.

umi_length.description: Length of the UMI sequence in base pairs.

umi_position.description: Position of the UMI within the sequencing read.

deduplication_method.description: Algorithm used to resolve PCR duplicates based on UMI sequences.

read_length.description: Length of each sequencing read in base pairs.

read_type.description: Whether reads are sequenced from one or both ends of the fragment.

sequencing_depth.description: Average number of reads per sample in millions.

raw_data_quality_control.description: Preprocessing and quality control steps applied to raw sequencing reads.

adapter_trimming.description: Tool used to remove sequencing adapter sequences from reads.

quality_filtering.description: Criteria or tool used to remove low-quality reads or bases.

qc_assessment_tool.description: Tool used for per-base quality assessment and QC reporting.

read_alignment.description: Read alignment or pseudoalignment to the reference genome or transcriptome.

annotation_version.description: Version of the transcript annotation used.

alignment_rate.description: Proportion of reads successfully aligned to the reference.

expression_quantification.description: Strategy for estimating gene or transcript expression levels from aligned reads.

level.description: Resolution at which expression is quantified.

count_summarization_method.description: Tool or method used to summarize aligned reads into expression counts.

normalization_aware.description: Whether the quantification step incorporates bias correction.

low_count_filtering.description: Criteria for removing lowly expressed genes prior to statistical testing.

min_counts_per_sample.description: Minimum read count required in a given sample for gene retention.

min_samples_with_min_counts.description: Minimum number of samples that must meet the count threshold.

tool.description: Tool used to apply low-count filtering.

normalization_method.description: Between-sample normalization to correct for library size and composition biases. RPKM/FPKM are within-sample metrics and not appropriate for DGE.

statistical_analysis.description: Statistical framework for differential expression testing, including model specification, batch correction, and significance criteria.

model_framework.description: Statistical model used for differential expression testing.

design_formula.description: Formula specifying experimental factors and covariates included in the model.

covariates.description: Additional variables included in the model to account for technical or biological variability.

dispersion_estimation.description: Method for estimating gene-wise overdispersion in count data.

batch_correction.description: Correction applied to remove technical variation introduced by experimental batches.

batch_variable.description: Name of the variable representing batch in the experimental design.

multiple_testing_correction.description: Method for controlling the false discovery rate across multiple hypothesis tests.

significance_thresholds.description: Thresholds used to declare a gene as differentially expressed.

adjusted_pvalue_threshold.description: Maximum adjusted p-value for a gene to be considered significant.

log2_fold_change_threshold.description: Minimum absolute log2 fold change required for biological significance.

output_artifacts.description: Files and visualizations generated by the workflow.

path.description: File path or URI where the artifact is stored.

format.description: File format of the output artifact.

reproducibility_metadata.description: Metadata required to reproduce the workflow and ensure FAIR compliance.

software_versions.description: Mapping of software tool names to their exact versions.

workflow_engine.description: Workflow management system used to orchestrate the pipeline.

computational_environment.description: Container or environment specification used for execution.

random_seed.description: Seed value used in stochastic steps to ensure reproducibility.

parameter_settings.description: Non-default parameter configurations applied across workflow tools.
